case_metadata.case_name: Austin Behavioral Hospital, LLC Operating as Cross Creek Hospital and Subject to It's Previously Filed Special Appearance, Acadia Healthcare Company, ,Inc. v. Eddie Lee Wilson, and Chester Jackson Sr., as Next Friend for Chester Jackson, Jr., Heather Martin A/N/F CCJ I, Heather Martin A/N/F CCJ, II, Pe'trecia Ray A/N/F D. H.
case_metadata.court: Court of Appeals, First District of Texas

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Jackson was admitted to Cross Creek Hospital after having a mental health episode. Soon after his admission, Jackson experienced severe agitation requiring hospital staff to physically restrain him and administer a combination of antipsychotic and sedative medications.
evidence_index.summary: Jackson admitted to hospital, experienced agitation, restrained and medicated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Jackson quit breathing almost immediately and went into cardiopulmonary arrest. Four hours after his admission to Cross Creek Hospital, Jackson was transferred to another hospital for emergency care.
evidence_index.summary: Jackson stopped breathing, went into arrest, transferred for emergency care.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Roppolo stated that the standard of care in this case required administering medications in a manner that did not compromise Jackson’s ability to breathe and ultimately caused his cardiopulmonary arrest.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined standard of care required safe medication administration.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Roppolo

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We hold that the trial court did not abuse its discretion by overruling the Providers’ objections to the expert report and denying the motion to dismiss challenging the adequacy of the expert report.
evidence_index.summary: Court upheld trial court's decision to overrule objections to expert report.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The report concedes that physical restraint can be part of a proper standard of care but opined that Cross Creek Hospital violated this standard of care by using a prone position.
evidence_index.summary: Court noted expert's opinion on improper use of prone restraint.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Roppolo concluded that if the hospital had taken appropriate actions, Jackson’s injuries could have been avoided.
evidence_index.summary: Expert concluded proper actions could have prevented injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Roppolo

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Jackson was placed in this hold for sixteen minutes from 5:36 p.m. until 5:52 p.m. Within two minutes of placing him in the hold, hospital staff tried to de-escalate Jackson, but he continued to be hostile and assaultive.
evidence_index.summary: Jackson restrained for 16 minutes, continued hostility.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Roppolo based her opinions on Jackson’s medical records, including records from Cross Creek Hospital, St. David’s Hospital, and Travis County EMS which transported Jackson between the hospitals.
evidence_index.summary: Expert reviewed medical records from multiple sources.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Roppolo

verdict_detected.key_phrase: We affirm the trial court’s order overruling the Providers’ objections to the expert report and motion to dismiss the lawsuit.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe agitation following mental health episode
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Jackson was admitted to Cross Creek Hospital after experiencing a mental health episode. Soon after admission, he became severely agitated.

requestables.name: Psychiatric Evaluation
requestables.description: Assessment by a psychiatrist to evaluate mental health status

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient experiencing severe agitation in hospital
decision_points.prompt: What is the appropriate method to manage the patient's agitation?

options.description: Administer antipsychotic and sedative medications with monitoring
options.clinical_reasoning: Medications were administered but led to respiratory compromise

options.description: Use verbal de-escalation techniques and monitor closely
options.clinical_reasoning: Safer initial approach to avoid respiratory compromise

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant administered medications without adequate monitoring

expected_action_court.description: Verbal de-escalation and monitoring should have been prioritized

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Medications compromised breathing, leading to arrest
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Safer management techniques should have been used

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient restrained due to severe agitation
decision_points.prompt: What is the appropriate method of physical restraint?

options.description: Use prone restraint with continuous monitoring
options.clinical_reasoning: Prone restraint used but led to respiratory compromise

options.description: Use supine restraint with continuous monitoring
options.clinical_reasoning: Safer position to avoid respiratory compromise

expected_action_court.description: Supine restraint should have been used

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Prone restraint increased risk of respiratory compromise
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Safer restraint techniques should have been used

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted to hospital following mental health episode

key_events.event: Patient admitted and experienced severe agitation

timeline_phases.name: Management of Agitation
timeline_phases.description: Patient managed for severe agitation with medications and restraint

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced cardiopulmonary arrest and was transferred for emergency care

key_findings: Breach of duty in medication administration and restraint method
Causation established - improper management led to respiratory arrest

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to safely manage agitation and use appropriate restraint
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Safer management and restraint could have prevented arrest

factual_timeline.event: Jackson admitted to Cross Creek Hospital

allegations.allegation: Improper medication administration and restraint method
